days.brief: EMSTesting.com Login and Orientation, Chapter 1: EMS Systems, Chapter 2: Workforce Safety and Wellness, Chapter 3: Medical, Legal, and Ethical Issues → PM: Group Testing - Chapters 1-3
days.debrief: Preview Day 2: Ch 4-6 Reading assignment: Chapters 4, 5, 6
objectives: Log in to EMSTesting.com, resolve any account issues
Summarize components of EMS Systems (Chapter 1)
Understand Workforce Safety and Wellness basics (Chapter 2)
Discuss Medical, Legal, and Ethical Issues in EMS (Chapter 3)
Complete learning style assessment
days.notes: Day 2 morning: quizzes on Ch 1, 2, 3 (10 questions each). Assign reading Ch 4-6.

morning.title: Roll Call, Introductions, Course Overview
morning.description: Instructor introduction and background; Student introductions; Course overview, syllabus review, academic policies (80% pass standard)

morning.title: EMSTesting.com Login and Orientation
morning.description: Students log in, fix any account issues; Walk through platform navigation; Complete a practice quiz to verify functionality

morning.title: Chapter 1: EMS Systems (Lecture)
morning.description: EMS history, levels of certification (EMR/EMT/AEMT/Paramedic); EMS system components, medical direction, QI; Where AEMT scope sits relative to EMT and Paramedic

morning.title: Chapter 2: Workforce Safety and Wellness (Lecture)
morning.description: Scene safety, standard precautions, BSI/PPE; Infectious disease exposure procedures; Stress management (acute, cumulative, PTSD); Lifting mechanics introduction

morning.title: Chapter 3: Medical, Legal, and Ethical Issues (Lecture)
morning.description: Scope of practice, duty to act; Consent types (expressed, implied, minor); Advance directives (DNR/POLST), HIPAA; Negligence, abandonment, documentation as legal protection

afternoon.title: Chapter 3 Continued - Case Studies
afternoon.description: Present 4 legal/ethical scenarios for class discussion; Competent adult refusing transport, minor without parent present; DNR with family doing CPR, social media implications

afternoon.title: Group Testing - Chapters 1-3
afternoon.description: Introduce EMSTesting group testing format; 15-20 questions projected, class discusses each question; Focus: certification levels, consent types, BSI, HIPAA

days.brief: Quizzes: Ch 1 + Ch 2 + Ch 3, Chapter 4: Communications and Documentation, Chapter 5: Medical Terminology, Chapter 6: Lifting and Moving Patients → PM: Group Testing - Chapters 4-6
days.debrief: Preview tomorrow: lab day, closed-toe shoes required Optional: PCR Narrative Activity if time allows
objectives: Complete quizzes on Chapters 1, 2, 3 from Day 1
Understand communications and documentation fundamentals (Chapter 4)
Learn medical terminology basics (Chapter 5)
Understand lifting and moving principles (Chapter 6 - lecture only, lab is Day 3)
days.notes: Day 3 morning: quizzes on Ch 4 (20q), Ch 5 (15q), Ch 6 (10q). Remind: closed-toe shoes for lab.

morning.title: Roll Call, Announcements
morning.description: Preview today's agenda

morning.title: Quizzes: Ch 1 (10q) + Ch 2 (10q) + Ch 3 (10q) - 30 questions
morning.description: Students complete individually on devices; ~1.5 min per question

morning.title: Quiz Review
morning.description: Review all questions on screen; Explain correct answers and why distractors are wrong

morning.title: Chapter 4: Communications and Documentation (Lecture)
morning.description: Therapeutic communication, verbal/nonverbal; Radio communication format and procedures; PCR components, legal importance of documentation

morning.title: Chapter 5: Medical Terminology (Lecture)
morning.description: Word parts (prefix, root, suffix); Body planes, directional terms; Abdominopelvic quadrants and regions

morning.title: Chapter 6: Lifting and Moving Patients (Lecture)
morning.description: Body mechanics principles; Emergency, urgent, non-urgent moves; Equipment overview: backboard, scoop, KED, stair chair, gurney

afternoon.title: Medical Terminology Worksheet
afternoon.description: Students work individually (~15 min) then review as class; Match prefix/suffix, identify body planes, directional terms

afternoon.title: Group Testing - Chapters 4-6
afternoon.description: 15-20 questions covering Ch 4-6 content; Focus: radio reports, PCR, body mechanics, patient positioning

days.brief: Quizzes: Ch 4 + Ch 5 + Ch 6, EMT Competency Checkoffs, Lab Prep and Safety Brief → PM: Lifting and Moving Lab
days.debrief: What went well, what needs more practice Reading assignment: Ch 7 (Human Body) - longest chapter in course
days.lab_note: Lab: Lifting & Moving | instructors needed: 1 | cadre: Beneficial: Patient actors for lifting
objectives: Complete quizzes on Chapters 4, 5, 6
Verify EMT-level competencies (BSI/PPE, NRB, BVM basics, vitals, O2 delivery)
Demonstrate proper lifting and moving techniques using backboard, scoop, KED, stair chair, and gurney
days.notes: Monday supplementary with Sun Kang: review week 1 content. Day 4: Ch 1-6 exam + start Ch 7 A&P.

morning.title: Roll Call, Announcements, Lab Day Preview
EMT Competency Checkoffs
morning.description: BSI/PPE donning and doffing; NRB setup and application; BVM basics - seal and squeeze technique; Vital signs - manual BP, pulse, RR, SpO2; Patient positioning, O2 delivery devices

morning.title: Lab Prep and Safety Brief
morning.description: Review station rotation plan; Safety: proper lifting mechanics, communicate before every lift

afternoon.title: Lifting and Moving Lab (~2 hours)
afternoon.description: Backboard with log-roll, scoop stretcher; KED application for seated patient; Stair chair operations; Gurney: loading, height adjustment, wheel lock; If cadre available: cadre serve as patient actors for realistic practice

days.brief: Ch 1-6 Sub-Module Exam, Chapter 7: Human Body - Part 1 → PM: Chapter 7: Parts 3-4
days.debrief: Reading: Ch 8-9
objectives: Complete Ch 1-6 Sub-Module Exam (~35 questions)
Begin comprehensive review of human body anatomy and physiology (Chapter 7)
Identify major body systems, anatomical terminology, and organ locations
days.notes: This is the heaviest lecture chapter in the course (240 min). Mid-day gap: Ben leaves ~1015, Jimi arrives ~noon.

morning.title: Ch 1-6 Sub-Module Exam (~35 questions)
morning.description: Students complete individually; ~1.5 min per question + review

morning.title: Chapter 7: Human Body - Part 1 (75 min)
morning.description: Anatomical terminology review (planes, directions, cavities); Musculoskeletal system (bones, muscles, joints); Integumentary system (skin layers, functions)

morning.title: Chapter 7: Part 2 - Self-Directed / A&P Worksheet
morning.description: Students work on A&P worksheet; Note: instructor transition period - cadre can proctor if available

afternoon.title: Chapter 7: Parts 3-4 (90 min)
afternoon.description: Respiratory system (airway anatomy, gas exchange); Cardiovascular system (heart, vessels, blood flow); Nervous system (CNS vs PNS, sympathetic vs parasympathetic); Remaining systems: endocrine, GI, urinary, reproductive

afternoon.title: A&P Worksheet Review / Body Systems Rapid Fire

days.brief: Quiz: Ch 7, Chapter 8: Pathophysiology, Chapter 9: Life Span Development → PM: Group Testing Ch 8-9
days.debrief: Reading: Ch 10 (Patient Assessment) - most important clinical chapter
objectives: Complete Ch 7 quiz
Understand cellular injury, acid-base balance, and disease pathology (Chapter 8)
Identify vital sign norms and assessment modifications by age group (Chapter 9)
days.notes: Day 6: Ch 10 Patient Assessment. Emphasize reading - everything builds on assessment.

morning.title: Chapter 8: Pathophysiology (Lecture - 90 min)
morning.description: Cellular environment, water balance, electrolytes; Cellular injury and death (hypoxia, ischemia, necrosis); Acid-base balance, fluid balance; Inflammation, infection, immunity basics

morning.title: Chapter 9: Life Span Development (Lecture - 45 min)
morning.description: Vital sign norms by age group; Pediatric vs adult anatomical differences; Geriatric physiological changes; Communication adjustments by age

morning.title: Life Span Case Studies (60 min)
morning.description: 6 scenarios representing different age groups; Discuss vitals, anatomical differences, communication approach

afternoon.title: A&P Review / Reinforcement Activities
afternoon.description: If cadre available: patient assessment intro scenarios with cadre as patients

days.brief: Quizzes: Ch 8 + Ch 9, Chapter 10: Patient Assessment, Assessment Demonstration → PM: Assessment Practice, Vital Signs Practice
days.debrief: Monday supplementary: review + exam prep. Ch 10 Assessment Exam is Tuesday.
objectives: Complete quizzes on Chapters 8-9
Master the patient assessment sequence: scene size-up through reassessment (Chapter 10)
Practice primary assessment, history taking (SAMPLE/OPQRST), and vital signs
days.notes: Ch 10 Assessment Exam on Day 7 (Tuesday). No daily quiz for Ch 10 - exam replaces it.

morning.title: Chapter 10: Patient Assessment (Lecture - 110 min)
morning.description: Scene size-up (safety, MOI/NOI, resources); Primary assessment (general impression, AVPU, ABCs); History taking (SAMPLE, OPQRST); Secondary assessment (head-to-toe vs focused); Vital signs, GCS, reassessment

morning.title: Assessment Demonstration
morning.description: Instructor walks through complete patient assessment on volunteer; Talk through every step aloud

afternoon.title: Assessment Practice (70 min)
afternoon.description: Students pair up, practice primary assessment sequence; If cadre available: cadre as patients at 3 stations (medical, trauma, AMS); If no cadre: students pair up, instructors circulate

afternoon.title: Vital Signs Practice (50 min)
afternoon.description: Manual BP (auscultation), pulse, RR, SpO2, pupils, skin assessment

days.brief: Ch 10 Assessment Exam, Chapter 12: Principles of Pharmacology, Chapter 13: Vascular Access → PM: Chapter 13: Finish, Med Admin Demonstration: IM, SQ, IN Routes, Med Admin Practice
days.debrief: Preview Day 8: IV manikin arms AM, live sticks PM

lab.name: Med Admin
lab.cadre: Helpful: Extra eyes on injection technique

days.lab_note: Lab: Med Admin | instructors needed: 1 | cadre: Helpful: Extra eyes on injection technique
objectives: Complete Ch 10 Assessment Exam (~45 questions)
Understand drug legislation, pharmacokinetics, and pharmacodynamics (Chapter 12)
Learn IV therapy indications, equipment, and site selection (Chapter 13)
Practice medication administration routes: IM, SQ, IN, nebulized
days.notes: Day 8: IV practice all day. Quizzes on Ch 12 + Ch 13 (40 questions). Pima cadre needed PM for live stick safety.

morning.title: Chapter 12: Principles of Pharmacology (Lecture - 80 min)
morning.description: Drug nomenclature, pharmacokinetics (ADME); Medication forms, routes of administration; SNHD formulary - medications in AEMT scope

morning.title: Chapter 13: Vascular Access (Lecture - 70 min of 90)
morning.description: IV therapy indications, fluid types (NS vs LR); IV equipment (catheters, admin sets, macro vs micro drip); IV site selection, insertion technique overview; Complications (infiltration, phlebitis, air embolism)

afternoon.title: Chapter 13: Finish (20 min)
afternoon.description: IO access overview, medication administration via IV

afternoon.title: Med Admin Demonstration: IM, SQ, IN Routes (30 min)
afternoon.description: Demonstrate each route with training equipment

afternoon.title: Med Admin Practice (70 min)
afternoon.description: IM injection technique (vastus lateralis, deltoid); SQ injection, med draws from vials; IN naloxone via mucosal atomization device; Nebulizer setup and administration

days.brief: Quizzes: Ch 12 + Ch 13, IV Manikin Arm Practice, IV Troubleshooting + Continued Arm Practice → PM: LIVE STICK IV Practice
days.lab_note: Lab: IV LIVE STICKS | instructors needed: 2-3 + cadre | cadre: Essential: Sharps safety - 1 per 5-6 students
objectives: Complete quizzes on Chapters 12 and 13
Demonstrate IV insertion technique on manikin arms
Perform supervised live stick IV access on training partners
days.notes: SHARPS SAFETY: Multiple instructors + cadre required for live sticks. Day 9: Sub-module exam + IV math.

morning.title: IV Manikin Arm Practice (110 min)
morning.description: Instructor demonstrates complete IV start; Tourniquet, site selection, angle, flash, advance catheter; Students practice on IV arms - goal: 3-4 starts each

morning.title: IV Troubleshooting + Continued Arm Practice (50 min)
Lunch - Sharps Safety Briefing Before PM Session
morning.description: Pima cadre arrives; Review consent protocol, sharps disposal, safety procedures

afternoon.title: LIVE STICK IV Practice (120 min)
afternoon.description: Supervised live sticks on training partners; 1 instructor/cadre per 5-6 students for sharps safety; Practice: IV start, connect admin set, set drip rate

days.brief: Ch 7-9, 12-13 Sub-Module Exam, IV Math and Drip Rate Calculations, EMT Competency Checkoffs → PM: Practice / Checkoffs Continued
objectives: Complete Ch 7-9, 12-13 Sub-Module Exam (~65 questions)
Master IV drip rate calculations (gtts/min formula)
Complete EMT competency checkoffs and IV skill integration
days.notes: Monday supplementary: review. Day 10: Ch 11 Airway + Ch 17 Respiratory (heavy lecture day).

morning.title: IV Math and Drip Rate Calculations (30 min)
morning.description: gtts/min = (Volume x Drop factor) / Time in minutes; Practice with macro (10 gtts/mL) and micro (60 gtts/mL) sets; Weight-based fluid bolus calculations

afternoon.title: Practice / Checkoffs Continued (120 min)
afternoon.description: IV skill integration scenarios if cadre available; Remaining EMT competency checkoffs

days.brief: Chapter 11: Airway Part 1, Chapter 11: Airway Part 2, Chapter 11: Airway Part 3, Chapter 17: Respiratory Part 1 → PM: Chapter 17: Respiratory Part 2, Chapter 17: Respiratory Part 3
days.debrief: Preview tomorrow: airway lab PM. Reading: review Ch 11 supraglottic insertion steps.
objectives: Master airway anatomy, assessment, and management techniques (Chapter 11)
Understand oxygen delivery devices, supraglottic airways, and CPAP
Identify respiratory emergencies and AEMT treatment protocols (Chapter 17)
days.notes: Heavy lecture day (~350 min). Day 11: Ch 14 Shock lecture AM + Airway Lab PM.

morning.title: Chapter 11: Airway Part 1 (60 min)
morning.description: Airway anatomy review (upper/lower); Airway assessment (look/listen/feel); Manual maneuvers (head-tilt chin-lift, jaw thrust); Basic adjuncts (OPA sizing/insertion, NPA sizing/insertion); Suctioning technique

morning.title: Chapter 11: Airway Part 2 (60 min)
morning.description: Oxygen delivery devices (NC, NRB, BVM); Pulse oximetry and capnography; BVM technique (1-person and 2-person)

morning.title: Chapter 11: Airway Part 3 (60 min)
morning.description: Supraglottic airways (King LT, i-gel) - indications, sizing, insertion; CPAP - indications, setup, application, monitoring; SNHD protocol for advanced airways at AEMT level

morning.title: Chapter 17: Respiratory Part 1 (50 min)
morning.description: Respiratory assessment, lung sounds (wheezes, crackles, rhonchi, stridor); Pulse oximetry and capnography interpretation; Respiratory distress vs failure vs arrest

afternoon.title: Chapter 17: Respiratory Part 2 (80 min)
afternoon.description: Asthma, COPD, pneumonia, pulmonary edema; Pulmonary embolism, pneumothorax (simple and tension); Epiglottitis, croup; For each: assessment findings and AEMT treatment

afternoon.title: Chapter 17: Respiratory Part 3 (40 min)
afternoon.description: Remaining conditions + treatment review; Albuterol nebulizer indications and setup

days.brief: Quizzes: Ch 11 + Ch 17, Chapter 14: Shock, Airway Lab Setup and Safety Brief → PM: Airway Management Lab
days.lab_note: Lab: Airway Lab | instructors needed: 1 | cadre: High value: Airway station supervision
objectives: Complete quizzes on Chapters 11 and 17
Understand shock pathophysiology, types, and AEMT treatment (Chapter 14)
Demonstrate airway management skills: OPA/NPA, BVM, suctioning, supraglottic, CPAP
days.notes: Day 12: Ch 15 BLS + Ch 18 Cardio lecture AM + BLS Lab PM.

morning.title: Chapter 14: Shock (Lecture - 120 min)
morning.description: Shock definition, perfusion triangle (pump/pipes/fluid); Types: hypovolemic, cardiogenic, distributive (anaphylactic/septic/neurogenic); Stages: compensated, decompensated, irreversible; AEMT treatment: O2, IV access, fluid resuscitation, positioning, rapid transport

morning.title: Airway Lab Setup and Safety Brief

afternoon.title: Airway Management Lab (~120 min)
afternoon.description: OPA/NPA sizing and insertion on manikins; Suctioning - rigid and flexible catheter technique; BVM ventilation (1-person and 2-person); Supraglottic airway insertion (King LT or i-gel) - minimum 3 times each; CPAP setup and application (if equipment available); If cadre available: additional station supervision

days.brief: Quiz: Ch 14, Chapter 15: BLS Resuscitation, Chapter 18: Cardiovascular Emergencies → PM: BLS Lab: CPR + AED + Team Resuscitation
days.debrief: Resuscitation Module Exam next Tuesday after weekend + Monday review
days.lab_note: Lab: BLS/CPR Lab | instructors needed: 1 | cadre: High value: Pit crew roles
objectives: Complete quiz on Chapter 14
Understand BLS resuscitation and team-based CPR concepts (Chapter 15)
Identify cardiovascular emergencies and AEMT treatment (Chapter 18)
Demonstrate high-quality CPR, AED use, and pit crew resuscitation
days.notes: Monday supplementary: Ch 15+18 quizzes + Resuscitation Module exam prep. Exam Day 13.

morning.title: Chapter 15: BLS Resuscitation (Lecture - 60 min)
morning.description: Chain of survival, CPR quality metrics (rate, depth, recoil, fraction); Adult/child/infant CPR differences; AED use, team-based resuscitation (pit crew model)

morning.title: Chapter 18: Cardiovascular Emergencies (Lecture - 90 min)
morning.description: ACS (angina vs MI), assessment, 12-lead indication; AEMT treatment: aspirin, nitroglycerin, O2, IV, rapid transport; Heart failure (left vs right sided); Hypertensive emergencies

morning.title: Group Testing Ch 15 + 18 / LVFR Pit Crew Review (50 min)

afternoon.title: BLS Lab: CPR + AED + Team Resuscitation (~120 min)
afternoon.description: High-quality CPR on manikins - rate, depth, recoil focus; AED application and integration with CPR; Team resuscitation scenarios - pit crew role rotations; If cadre available: cadre fill team roles for realistic crew dynamics

days.brief: Resuscitation Module Exam, Chapter 26: Trauma Overview, Chapter 27: Bleeding → PM: Chapter 27: Finish, Chapter 28: Soft-Tissue Injuries
objectives: Complete Resuscitation Module Exam (~75 questions, covers Ch 11, 14, 15, 17, 18)
Understand trauma as disease, mechanisms of injury, and energy transfer (Chapter 26)
Identify hemorrhage types and control methods (Chapter 27)
Understand soft-tissue injury classification and burn management (Chapter 28)
days.notes: Day 14: Ch 29-30 lectures + Stop the Bleed Lab PM.

morning.title: Chapter 26: Trauma Overview (Lecture - 50 min)
morning.description: MOI (blunt vs penetrating), energy transfer; MVA biomechanics, blast injuries; Trauma scoring, trauma center designation

morning.title: Chapter 27: Bleeding (Lecture - 30 min of 40)
morning.description: Blood volume, types of bleeding (arterial/venous/capillary); External hemorrhage control methods

afternoon.title: Chapter 28: Soft-Tissue Injuries (Lecture - 75 min)
afternoon.description: Closed injuries, open injuries; Burn classification (depth, extent - rule of nines); Burn management, dressing and bandaging principles

afternoon.title: Group Testing Ch 26-28 / Trauma Scenarios (45 min)

days.brief: Quizzes: Ch 26 + Ch 27 + Ch 28, Chapter 29: Face and Neck Injuries, Chapter 30: Head and Spine Injuries → PM: Stop the Bleed / Hemorrhage Control Lab
days.lab_note: Lab: Stop the Bleed | instructors needed: 1 | cadre: Beneficial: Bleeding scenarios
objectives: Complete quizzes on Chapters 26, 27, 28
Identify facial, eye, and neck injury management (Chapter 29)
Understand TBI, spinal cord injury, and immobilization indications (Chapter 30)
Demonstrate tourniquet application, wound packing, and hemostatic gauze use
days.notes: Day 15: Ch 31-32 lectures + C-Spine Lab PM.

morning.title: Chapter 29: Face and Neck Injuries (Lecture - 70 min)
morning.description: Eye injuries, ear/nose injuries, dental injuries; Neck injuries (vascular, airway, spine); Airway management in facial trauma

morning.title: Chapter 30: Head and Spine Injuries (Lecture - 80 min)
morning.description: TBI (concussion, contusion, epidural/subdural hematoma); Spinal anatomy and injury patterns; Spinal assessment, immobilization techniques

afternoon.title: Stop the Bleed / Hemorrhage Control Lab (~120 min)
afternoon.description: Tourniquet application (CAT - high and tight) - minimum 3 times each; Wound packing (junctional and extremity wounds); Hemostatic gauze application; Direct pressure techniques; If cadre available: cadre as bleeding patients for scenario realism

days.brief: Quizzes: Ch 29 + Ch 30, Chapter 31: Chest Injuries, Chapter 32: Abdominal and GU Injuries → PM: C-Spine Immobilization Lab
days.debrief: Monday supplementary: trauma quizzes + review
days.lab_note: Lab: C-Spine | instructors needed: 1 | cadre: Beneficial: C-spine scenarios
objectives: Complete quizzes on Chapters 29 and 30
Identify chest injury types and management (Chapter 31)
Understand abdominal and GU trauma assessment (Chapter 32)
Demonstrate C-collar application, backboard immobilization, and helmet removal
days.notes: Day 16: Ch 33-34 + Splinting Lab PM. Last day of Pima summer semester.

morning.title: Chapter 31: Chest Injuries (Lecture - 90 min)
morning.description: Rib fractures, flail chest, pneumothorax types; Hemothorax, cardiac tamponade; Chest seal application, positioning, rapid transport

morning.title: Chapter 32: Abdominal and GU Injuries (Lecture - 60 min)
morning.description: Solid vs hollow organ injuries; Evisceration management; GU trauma assessment and management

afternoon.title: C-Spine Immobilization Lab (~120 min)
afternoon.description: Manual inline stabilization; C-collar sizing and application; Supine backboard immobilization with log-roll; Seated patient with KED; Motorcycle helmet removal (2-person technique); If cadre available: cadre as patients for realistic scenarios

days.brief: Quizzes: Ch 31 + Ch 32, Chapter 33: Orthopedic Injuries, Chapter 34: Environmental Emergencies → PM: Chapter 34: Finish, Splinting Lab
days.debrief: Monday: trauma quizzes + Trauma Module exam prep
days.lab_note: Lab: Splinting | instructors needed: 1 | cadre: Beneficial: Splinting scenarios
objectives: Complete quizzes on Chapters 31 and 32
Understand fracture types, assessment (6 Ps), and splinting principles (Chapter 33)
Identify heat, cold, drowning, and envenomation emergencies (Chapter 34)
Demonstrate splint application including traction splint
days.notes: Pima off week - Ben available full day. Trauma Module Exam is Day 17 (Wednesday).

morning.title: Chapter 33: Orthopedic Injuries (Lecture - 95 min)
morning.description: Fracture types, dislocations, sprains/strains; Assessment: 6 Ps (pain, paralysis, paresthesia, pulselessness, pallor, pressure); Splinting principles, PMS checks before and after; Pelvic fractures, compartment syndrome, crush syndrome

morning.title: Chapter 34: Environmental Emergencies (Lecture - 80 min of 120)
morning.description: Heat emergencies (cramps, exhaustion, heat stroke); Cold emergencies (hypothermia, frostbite); Drowning, diving emergencies, altitude

afternoon.title: Chapter 34: Finish (40 min)
afternoon.description: Bites and envenomations, lightning injuries

afternoon.title: Splinting Lab (~80 min)
afternoon.description: SAM splint application (forearm fracture); Rigid splint (lower leg); Sling and swathe (shoulder/clavicle); Traction splint application (femur fracture) - minimum 2 times each; PMS check before and after each application; If cadre available: cadre as patients for fracture scenarios

days.brief: Quizzes: Ch 33 + Ch 34, Trauma Module Exam → PM: Chapter 16: Medical Overview, Chapter 19: Neurologic Emergencies
objectives: Complete Trauma Module Exam (~65 questions, covers Ch 26-34)
Understand medical patient assessment approach (Chapter 16)
Identify stroke, seizures, and AMS (Chapter 19)
days.notes: Day 18: Ch 20-22 + medical scenarios PM.

afternoon.title: Chapter 16: Medical Overview (Lecture - 45 min)
afternoon.description: Medical vs trauma patients; Medical patient assessment approach; Infectious disease overview and standard precautions

afternoon.title: Chapter 19: Neurologic Emergencies (Lecture - 60 min)
afternoon.description: Stroke (ischemic vs hemorrhagic, FAST/RACE scales, time windows); Seizures (types, status epilepticus); AMS (differential diagnosis), syncope

days.brief: Quizzes: Ch 16 + Ch 19, Chapter 20: GI and Urologic Emergencies, Chapter 21: Endocrine and Hematologic, Chapter 22: Immunologic Emergencies → PM: Group Testing Ch 20-22
objectives: Complete quizzes on Chapters 16 and 19
Understand GI and urologic emergency assessment and management (Chapter 20)
Identify diabetic emergencies and glucometer use (Chapter 21)
Understand anaphylaxis pathophysiology and epinephrine administration (Chapter 22)
days.notes: Day 19: Ch 23-25 (finish all medical content).

morning.title: Chapter 20: GI and Urologic Emergencies (Lecture - 90 min)
morning.description: Acute abdomen assessment; Specific conditions by quadrant/location; GI bleeding (upper vs lower), kidney stones, UTI

morning.title: Chapter 21: Endocrine and Hematologic (Lecture - 60 min)
morning.description: Diabetes (Type 1 vs Type 2); Hypoglycemia (assessment, oral glucose, glucagon); Hyperglycemia and DKA (Kussmaul respirations); Sickle cell crisis, clotting disorders

morning.title: Chapter 22: Immunologic Emergencies (Lecture - 40 min)
morning.description: Allergic reactions vs anaphylaxis; Anaphylaxis treatment algorithm (epi + airway + IV + albuterol)

afternoon.title: Medical Scenario Practice (90 min)
afternoon.description: If cadre available: 4 stations (stroke/AMS, diabetic, anaphylaxis, abdominal pain); If no cadre: facilitated case study discussion - same scenarios

days.brief: Quizzes: Ch 20 + Ch 21 + Ch 22, Chapter 23: Toxicology, Chapter 24: Psychiatric Emergencies → PM: Chapter 25: Gynecologic Emergencies
days.debrief: All medical content complete. Medical Module Exam Day 22 (Tuesday).
objectives: Complete quizzes on Chapters 20, 21, 22
Identify toxidromes and overdose management including naloxone (Chapter 23)
Understand psychiatric emergencies and de-escalation (Chapter 24)
Identify gynecologic emergencies and trauma-informed care (Chapter 25)
days.notes: Heavy quiz morning (60 questions). Monday supplementary: Ch 23-25 quizzes + Medical Module exam prep.

morning.title: Chapter 23: Toxicology (Lecture - 75 min)
morning.description: Toxicology assessment approach; Toxidromes (sympathomimetic, cholinergic, anticholinergic, opioid, sedative); Naloxone administration (IN/IV/IM); Carbon monoxide, organophosphates

morning.title: Chapter 24: Psychiatric Emergencies (Lecture - 45 min)
morning.description: Behavioral emergencies, psychiatric conditions; Suicidal/homicidal ideation assessment; Scene safety, de-escalation techniques

afternoon.title: Chapter 25: Gynecologic Emergencies (Lecture - 50 min)
afternoon.description: Vaginal bleeding (ectopic pregnancy, miscarriage); Sexual assault (evidence preservation, trauma-informed care); PID, ovarian torsion

afternoon.title: Medical Case Studies / Group Testing Ch 23-25 (60 min)

days.brief: Quizzes: Ch 23 + Ch 24 + Ch 25 → PM: Integrated Medical Scenarios
days.debrief: Medical Module Exam Tuesday. Study all medical chapters.
objectives: Complete outstanding chapter quizzes if not done Monday
Review all medical module content through group testing
Practice integrated medical patient scenarios
days.notes: Day 21: Ch 35 OB lecture + OB Lab PM.

morning.title: Quizzes: Ch 23 (20q) + Ch 24 (15q) + Ch 25 (10q) if not done Monday
Medical Module Review - Group Testing (100 min)
morning.description: Comprehensive questions across Ch 16, 19-25; Focus on commonly missed areas from quizzes

morning.title: Medical Review Continued + Case Studies (110 min)
morning.description: Differential diagnosis practice; Complex patient presentations requiring field impression

afternoon.title: Integrated Medical Scenarios (90 min)
afternoon.description: If cadre available: 4 stations (tox/OD, psych crisis, diabetic, chest pain); If no cadre: facilitated case studies - same scenarios; Full assessment, treatment, transport decision for each

afternoon.title: Skill Checkoffs - Catch Up Outstanding (40 min)

days.brief: Chapter 35: OB and Neonatal Care - Parts 1-2 → PM: OB Delivery / NRP Lab
objectives: Understand pregnancy physiology, stages of labor, and normal delivery (Chapter 35)
Identify delivery complications and management
Demonstrate neonatal resuscitation (NRP basics)
Practice delivery procedure on manikin
days.notes: Day 22: Medical Module Exam + Ch 36 Pediatrics begins.

morning.title: Chapter 35: OB and Neonatal Care - Parts 1-2 (160 min)
morning.description: Pregnancy anatomy and physiology; Stages of labor, normal delivery procedure step by step; Complications: breech, prolapsed cord, nuchal cord, shoulder dystocia, meconium; Postpartum hemorrhage, preeclampsia/eclampsia; Neonatal assessment (APGAR), NRP basics; Include break ~0840-0850

morning.title: OB Scenarios Discussion (70 min)
morning.description: Walk through delivery complications as group; Nuchal cord management, breech support, prolapsed cord positioning

morning.title: Group Testing Ch 35 / Delivery Review (50 min)

afternoon.title: OB Delivery / NRP Lab (~90 min)
afternoon.description: Normal delivery on manikin - each student performs minimum 2 times; Neonatal resuscitation: stimulate, dry, warm, suction, BVM, compressions; If cadre available: cadre as laboring patient for scenario realism

afternoon.title: Peds Preview Discussion / Additional Practice (40 min)

days.brief: Medical Module Exam, Chapter 35: OB Part 1 → PM: Chapter 35: Part 2, Group Testing Ch 35 / OB Discussion
days.debrief: Preview tomorrow: OB Lab + birth scenarios
objectives: Complete Medical Module Exam (~65 questions, covers Ch 16, 19-25)
Understand pregnancy physiology, stages of labor, and delivery (Chapter 35)
Identify delivery complications and neonatal resuscitation basics
days.notes: Day 23: OB Lab with delivery practice and birth scenarios.

morning.title: Chapter 35: OB Part 1 (80 min)
morning.description: Pregnancy anatomy and physiology; Stages of labor, normal delivery step by step

afternoon.title: Chapter 35: Part 2 (80 min)
afternoon.description: Delivery complications: breech, prolapsed cord, nuchal cord; Postpartum hemorrhage, preeclampsia/eclampsia; APGAR scoring, NRP basics

afternoon.title: Group Testing Ch 35 / OB Discussion (50 min)

days.brief: Quiz: Ch 35 → PM: OB Delivery Lab
days.debrief: Preview: Pediatrics Day 1
days.lab_note: Lab: OB/NRP Lab | instructors needed: 1 | cadre: High value: OB delivery scenarios
objectives: Complete quiz on Chapter 35
Demonstrate normal delivery procedure on manikin
Practice neonatal resuscitation (NRP)
Work through delivery complication scenarios
days.notes: Day 24: Ch 36 Pediatrics begins - 320 min spread across 2 days.

morning.title: OB Review and Delivery Walkthrough (100 min)
morning.description: Review delivery steps; Group Testing Ch 35

morning.title: NRP Review and Practice (110 min)
morning.description: Neonatal assessment, APGAR practice; NRP algorithm on infant manikins - minimum 2 times each

afternoon.title: OB Delivery Lab (~90 min)
afternoon.description: Normal delivery on manikin - minimum 2 times each; Complication scenarios: nuchal cord, postpartum hemorrhage; If cadre available: cadre as laboring patient

days.brief: Chapter 36: Peds Part 1, Chapter 36: Peds Part 2 → PM: Peds Scenarios Continued
days.debrief: ~170 of 320 min peds done. Day 25 finishes peds.
objectives: Understand pediatric assessment triangle (Chapter 36)
Identify pediatric vital sign norms by age
Recognize pediatric respiratory emergencies
Apply concepts through case studies or scenarios
days.notes: Labor Day Monday (Sep 7) - no Sun Kang. Day 25: Peds Day 2.

morning.title: Chapter 36: Peds Part 1 (60 min)
morning.description: Pediatric assessment triangle; Anatomy and physiology differences; Vital signs by age

morning.title: Chapter 36: Peds Part 2 (70 min)
morning.description: Pediatric assessment approach; Respiratory emergencies: croup, epiglottitis, bronchiolitis, asthma; Pediatric seizures

morning.title: Peds Case Studies / Scenarios (110 min)
morning.description: Group-led case studies covering AM content; If cadre available: scenario stations with cadre as parents/patients

afternoon.title: Peds Scenarios Continued (90 min)
afternoon.description: Additional case studies or scenario stations; Broselow tape intro and practice; If cadre available: pediatric scenario stations

afternoon.title: Group Testing - Peds Part 1-2 Review (40 min)

days.brief: Chapter 36: Peds Part 3, Chapter 36: Peds Part 4 → PM: Group Testing - Peds Comprehensive
days.debrief: All peds done (320 min across 2 days).
objectives: Understand pediatric shock and fluid resuscitation (Chapter 36)
Identify pediatric trauma patterns and abuse indicators
Understand pediatric cardiac arrest and medication dosing
Apply concepts through case studies or scenarios
days.notes: Day 26: Ch 37 Geriatrics + Ch 38 Special Challenges.

morning.title: Chapter 36: Peds Part 3 (90 min)
morning.description: Pediatric shock, trauma, non-accidental trauma; SIDS

morning.title: Chapter 36: Peds Part 4 (60 min)
morning.description: Medication dosing, Broselow tape; Pediatric cardiac arrest; Consent for minors, family support

morning.title: Peds Case Studies / Scenarios (90 min)
morning.description: Group-led or scenario stations covering AM content; If cadre available: peds trauma, abuse recognition scenarios

afternoon.title: Peds Scenarios Continued (90 min)
afternoon.description: If cadre available: full pediatric patient contact scenarios; If no cadre: additional case studies, Broselow practice

days.brief: Quiz: Ch 36, Chapter 37: Geriatric Emergencies, Chapter 38: Special Challenges, Group Testing Ch 37-38 → PM: Skill Checkoffs
objectives: Complete quiz on Chapter 36
Identify geriatric assessment modifications (Chapter 37)
Understand special patient populations (Chapter 38)
Practice integrated special population scenarios
days.notes: Day 27: Special Populations review + scenarios.

morning.title: Chapter 37: Geriatric Emergencies (80 min)
morning.description: Physiological changes of aging, polypharmacy; Atypical presentations, elder abuse

morning.title: Chapter 38: Special Challenges (50 min)
morning.description: Tracheostomies, bariatric patients; Developmental disabilities, chronic conditions

afternoon.title: Integrated Special Pop Scenarios (90 min)
afternoon.description: If cadre: mixed peds/OB/geri scenario stations; If no cadre: group-led case studies

days.brief: Quiz: Ch 37 + Ch 38 → PM: Final Skill Checkoffs
days.debrief: Special Pop Exam next Tuesday.
objectives: Complete quizzes on Chapters 37 and 38
Review all special populations content
Practice integrated scenarios
Complete final skill checkoffs
days.notes: Monday supplementary: Ch 37-38 quizzes + Special Pop exam prep.

morning.title: Special Pop Review - Group Testing (110 min)
morning.description: Comprehensive questions across Ch 35-38

afternoon.title: Integrated Special Pop Scenarios (90 min)
afternoon.description: If cadre: 3-4 stations (peds, trauma, geri, OB); If no cadre: group-led case studies

days.brief: Special Populations Module Exam, Chapter 39: Transport Operations, Chapter 40: Extrication, Rescue, Hazmat, Chapter 41: Incident Management → PM: Chapter 41: Finish, Chapter 42: Terrorism and Disaster, MCI Tabletop Exercise, Group Testing Ch 39-42
days.debrief: All 42 chapters complete. EMS Ops Exam tomorrow.
objectives: Complete Special Populations Module Exam (~55 questions)
Understand transport operations and air medical (Chapter 39)
Identify extrication, hazmat, and rescue roles (Chapter 40)
Understand ICS and START/JumpSTART triage (Chapter 41)
Identify WMD types and terrorism response (Chapter 42)
days.notes: Day 29: EMS Ops Exam + comprehensive review + final scenarios.

afternoon.title: MCI Tabletop Exercise (40 min)
afternoon.description: Students triage patients using START algorithm

days.brief: EMS Ops Module Exam → PM: Final Scenario Practice
days.debrief: Final Exam tomorrow. Rest and trust your training.
objectives: Complete EMS Operations Module Exam (~35 questions)
Review all modules through comprehensive group testing
Practice integrated patient scenarios
Complete remaining skill checkoffs
days.notes: FINAL EXAM TOMORROW.

morning.title: Comprehensive Review - All Modules (100 min)
morning.description: Group testing, focus weak areas; High-yield NREMT topics

afternoon.title: Final Scenario Practice (90 min)
afternoon.description: If cadre: 4 stations (medical, trauma, peds, cardiac arrest); If no cadre: practice exam format review

days.brief: Comprehensive Final Exam → PM: NREMT Preparation Briefing, Course Evaluations + Admin Closeout
days.debrief: Final words, congratulations, dismiss
objectives: Complete Comprehensive Final Exam (~100-150 questions)
Understand NREMT exam format and preparation
Complete course evaluations and admin closeout
Celebrate course completion
days.notes: Students must pass the final and complete all requirements.

morning.title: Roll Call, Final Exam Prep
morning.description: Confirm devices; Exam protocol: individual work, no talking, no phones

morning.title: Comprehensive Final Exam (~100-150 questions)
morning.description: Students complete individually

afternoon.title: NREMT Preparation Briefing (60 min)
afternoon.description: Exam format (CAT); Registration, scheduling, tips
